description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, dark metallic blade and a long, textured wooden handle. The blade features two wide, crescent-shaped cutting edges, each with a slightly beveled profile and a central ridge for added strength. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped with a visible grip texture, and tapers slightly toward a rounded pommel at the end, which appears reinforced with metal. The axe head is securely mounted to the handle with a reinforced collar, and the overall design suggests both durability and a focus on heavy-duty use. The metal surfaces show a matte, slightly worn finish, while the wood has a natural, slightly reddish-brown hue. No visible markings or labels are present, giving the axe a utilitarian, possibly hand-forged appearance.
description: A double-headed battle axe with a dark metal blade and a long, textured wooden handle, featuring a robust, utilitarian design. The blade is wide and crescent-shaped, while the handle is reinforced and grippy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical double-bladed head and the rounded pommel at the base of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the handle and the thickness and curvature of the double-edged blade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the axe head and the handle's grip texture, with the pommel clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the blade's profile and the full length of the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: Long, cylindrical wooden handle with a slight taper and visible grip texture, straight with a rounded metal-reinforced pommel.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped steel blade with two wide cutting edges and a central ridge, no beard, and a reinforced collar at the mount.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and collared mounting with visible reinforcement at the blade-handle junction.
